description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service and defines the stages build, test, release, and deploy. In the build stage add a build job that runs `docker info` and `docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com`, then builds the image with `docker build -t $CONTAINER_CI_IMAGE .` and pushes it with `docker push $CONTAINER_CI_IMAGE`. In the test stage add a test job that also runs the same before‑script, pulls the image with `docker pull $CONTAINER_CI_IMAGE` and executes it in a testing environment via `docker run -e APP_ENV=testing $CONTAINER_CI_IMAGE`. In the release stage add a release-image-dev job that runs only on the develop branch, runs the Docker login steps, pulls $CONTAINER_CI_IMAGE, tags it as $CONTAINER_DEV_IMAGE, and pushes it, and a release-image-prod job that runs only on the master branch, pulls $CONTAINER_CI_IMAGE, tags it as $CONTAINER_RELEASE_IMAGE, and pushes it. Finally, in the deploy stage add a deploy-dev job using the cdrx/rancher-gitlab-deploy image that runs only on develop and executes `upgrade --environment dev --stack acropole-dev --service bot`, and a deploy-prod job that runs only on master and runs `upgrade --environment dev --stack acropole --service bot`.
